config.model: FusionModel (3B StructureSequenceEncoder + 3A ChartCNN, late-fused by a 2->8->1 MLP)
config.fusion: late (each branch -> scalar score; MLP over [score_3b, score_3a])
config.purge: canonical: partition_for_role(role='miner') (train-only panel, validation/sealed rows excluded) + validate_no_holdout_leakage fail-closed + purge_labels_at_boundary (cross-split-boundary labels dropped)
config.temporal_split_discipline: train-only mining panel; no validation/sealed rows; 21d labels crossing a split boundary purged

eval.eval_method: within-train fit/OOS year-block split with P3-A3 fit→OOS year-boundary purge embargo; per-OOS-date cross-sectional Spearman rank-IC of fused score vs 21d close-to-close forward return; paired t-test of per-date IC vs baseline
eval.turnover_penalty: monthly top-8 name turnover = 0.857; implied cost drag ~3.09%/yr at 30bp/side

root_cause: 3C late-fusion OOS rank-IC is significantly BELOW the 126d momentum baseline. Late fusion takes the two branch scores ([3B segment-sequence score, 3A GAF-image score]) and combines them with a small MLP; it cannot recover cross-sectional rank signal that neither branch carries. Both branches (P3·R2 / P3·R4) individually underperformed the same momentum baseline, so their late combination has no orthogonal signal to fuse — the fusion MLP only re-weights two already-dominated scores.
notes: Phase 3 chart-native late-fusion attempt. Within-train fit/OOS split — no validation/sealed data read. Date-subsampled (stride 3) for RAM, aligned (seg,img) per sample. ref_3b/3a IC are the recorded P3·R2/P3·R4 single-branch OOS rank-IC on the same protocol.
